frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment:
Meena: Nowadays girls are talking as if every man outside is dangerous. That is also not fair, no? So many decent men travel in the same train and go to work quietly.
Kavitha: I am only saying women have to be careful.
Meena: Careful is fine, but this constant fear and doubt about all men is too much. Men also face problems outside; we cannot live by assuming the worst about everyone.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it minimizes women's safety concerns by reframing their fear as unfair generalization against men. It encodes the 'not all men' dismissal and the deflection to men's problems instead of engaging with why women are wary.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 54. Married. A homemaker in Chennai. Your daughter in law Kavitha has just been offered a job that requires her to travel by local train and walk 10 minutes from the station to the office every day. She is excited about the job. You are sitting with your son and Kavitha at dinner after she shared the news. You are giving your opinion on the job and specifically on the commute and what can happen to women who travel alone and whether the salary is worth the risk.
